channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s 3rd nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama inmate convicted in the 1994 killing of a female hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gunmen fire on vehicles carrying Shiites in northwest Pakistan, killing at least 42
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Gunmen opened fire on vehicles carrying Shiite Muslims in Pakistan's restive northwest on Thursday, killing at least 42 people, including six women, and wounding 20 others in one of the region's deadliest such attacks in recent years, police said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prolific Mexican cartel leader allegedly faked his own death to live 'life of luxury' in California
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader, who peddled thousands of kilograms of illegal drugs to the U.S., was killed by a business associate for "lying" — or at least that's what the public was told.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KREX 5

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs. Trump was bruised and hampered by internal squabbles during his initial term in office.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UN nuclear agency’s board condemns Iran for the 2nd time this year for failing to fully cooperate
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The U.N. nuclear watchdog’s board on Thursday condemned Iran for failing to cooperate fully with the agency, the second time it has done so in just five months.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump picks Pam Bondi for next attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump has named former Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi as his next nominee to lead the Department of Justice (DOJ). Bondi was a swift pick just hours after former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew himself from consideration for the role.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people and unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pathologist disputes finding that Marine veteran's chokehold caused subway rider's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For roughly six minutes, Jordan Neely was pinned to a subway floor in a chokehold that ended with him lying still. But that's not what killed him, a forensic pathologist testified Thursday in defense of the military-trained commuter charged with killing Neely. Dr.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Known St. Louis-based Neo-Nazi group takes credit for Ohio disturbance
item.description: We get close to the line of whether it’s freedom of speech or the attempt to intimidate,” St. Louis City NAACP President Adolphus Pruitt said.
item.dc:creator: Max Diekneite, NewsNation

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US towns plunge into debates about fluoride in water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For about 50 years, adding cavity-preventing fluoride to drinking water was a popular public health measure in Yorktown, a leafy town north of New York City. But in September, the town’s supervisor used his emergency powers to stop the practice.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will lead the Republican Governors Association for the next year, the group announced Wednesday, just weeks after the party secured governorships in eight out of 11 state races up for grabs in the election earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New study shows voting for Native Americans is harder than ever
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY, Okla. (AP) — A new study has found that systemic barriers to voting on tribal lands contribute to substantial disparities in Native American turnout, particularly for presidential elections.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Students can now apply for federal college aid. Officials hope for a smoother rollout this year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal FAFSA form used to apply for college financial aid opened to high school seniors nationwide on Thursday, a year after a disastrous rollout by the Biden administration delayed the application for months.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mired in delays, US ambassador to Mexico pushes for new border crossing to get done
item.description: "We need a modern new border crossing, Otay Mesa East, it’s not completely done yet, but we’re getting close, we’re going to have it here in the next couple of years.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: The total number of women who are state lawmakers has once again reached new heights, following a trend up in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, NewsNation

item.title: Daniel Penny trial: Marine vet could testify to support justified defense claim, NYC defense lawyer says
item.description: A prominent New York City defense lawyer predicts Daniel Penny will take the witness stand in his own defense at his trial over the death of Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Storm dumps record rain and heavy snow on Northern California. Many in Seattle still without power
item.description: A major storm moving through Northern California has toppled trees and dropped heavy snow and record rain after knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of people in Washington and Oregon.
item.dc:creator: Noah Berger And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge keeps death penalty a possibility for man charged in killings of 4 Idaho students
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — The death penalty will remain a possibility for a man charged with murder in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Carcass of endangered fin whale washes up near Alaska's largest city
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An endangered fin whale that washed up near a coastal trail in Alaska's largest city has attracted curious onlookers while biologists seek answers as to what caused the animal's death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: 3 killed in series of random stabbings in New York City
item.description: A man is accused of fatally stabbing three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Debris falls from roof at home of Cowboys before game against Texans
item.description: A piece of the roof at the home of the Dallas Cowboys fell to the field while the retractable portion of it was being opened at least three hours before a 34-10 loss to Houston on Monday night, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sister of ex-Marine charged in subway chokehold death testifies to his 'calm' spirit and patriotism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The older sister of Daniel Penny, the former U.S. Marine charged with fatally choking a homeless man aboard a Manhattan subway, told jurors Monday that her brother was a “calm, soft-spirited person” with a reputation for honesty and integrity.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: Sixty-five percent of those who get news from social media say influencers have bettered their understanding of current events and civic issues.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Malcom X estate alleges DOJ, FBI, CIA, NYPD played role in events leading to assassination
item.description: The estate of Malcolm X is suing the federal government, accusing it of playing a role in the 1965 assassination of the civil rights leader.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9
